claim: Added global keyboard shortcuts (Space=pause, 1/2/4=speed, m=map, f=family) via a pure, unit-tested keyboardShortcut() mapper wired into UIOverlay; gave the icon-only sound button an aria-label; added :focus-visible outlines to buttons/select; raised chronicle year-stamp text to meet WCAG AA contrast. No src/sim changes.
notes: Shortcuts ignore keypresses while focus is in INPUT/SELECT/TEXTAREA and when Alt/Ctrl/Meta is held; Space's default scroll is prevented. The speed-button highlight was refactored into applySpeed() so click and keyboard stay in sync (behaviour for clicks unchanged). The only truly icon-only button is the sound toggle (pause/speed/map/family/save/load/new all carry visible text labels); it received aria-label="Toggle sound". Shortcut keys are documented in-UI via title attributes on the relevant buttons. Contrast: audited key text — body/ink, ink-dim, accent, goal, and log message text already pass WCAG AA on the panel background; the chronicle year-stamps (.ev span) were the genuinely-failing key informational text (~2.45:1) and were lightened #7a6850 -> #b09a7e (~4.85:1). Intentionally de-emphasised non-key text (locked techs, future era pips/language steps) was left as designed. Tests added in src/ui/shortcuts.test.ts (DOM-free, runs under the existing node test env; no jsdom dependency introduced). ran: npm test (38 passed), npm run build (passed). ranSim skipped: UI-only, no simulation/balance code touched.
